sourceMovie.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Ant-Man and the Wasp (2018) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Infinity War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Ant-Man and the Wasp excels in action, Avengers: Infinity War (2018) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014) is the natural next step after Ant-Man and the Wasp: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and adventure elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Avengers: Endgame
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Ant-Man and the Wasp who respond to its action will find Avengers: Endgame (2019) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Captain America: Civil War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Ant-Man and the Wasp fans keep landing on Captain America: Civil War (2016) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Iron Man 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Iron Man 2 draws from the same action well as Ant-Man and the Wasp, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2010 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Spider-Man: No Way Home (2021) captures the action appeal of Ant-Man and the Wasp without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Spider-Man: No Way Home at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: Captain Marvel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Ant-Man and the Wasp — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into Captain Marvel (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Guardians of the Galaxy Vol. 3 (2023) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Ant-Man and the Wasp rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ant-Man and the Wasp: Quantumania (2023) is the kind of film Ant-Man and the Wasp fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Flash
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Flash shares Ant-Man and the Wasp's gift for action — the 2023 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ant-Man and the Wasp?
faq.answer: Try Avengers: Infinity War, Guardians of the Galaxy, Avengers: Endgame—each shares DNA with Ant-Man and the Wasp while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ant-Man and the Wasp?
faq.answer: Ant-Man and the Wasp (2018) is best described as Action, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ant-Man and the Wasp part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ant-Man Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
